default_title: Effective Smeared-Transfer Closure in a Clustered DK Sector
alternative_titles: Phase-Corridor-Controlled Texture Transition in a Clustered DK Branch
Reduction and One-Sided Closure of a Clustered DK Interaction Sector

figures.paper_role: family non-generality and topology survival summary
figures.caption_draft: Clustered and non-clustered comparison summary from the mechanism non-generality pass. The clustered baseline retains the braid label, mild clustered degradation shifts to transfer-smeared behavior, and non-clustered families do not recover a robust braid sector.
figures.usage_note: May be used as a single figure with two panels or as one unchanged primary figure plus one supporting inset.

figures.paper_role: effective reduction and one-sided phase closure
figures.caption_draft: Reduced-model and phase-closure summary for the clustered sector. The surrogate reproduces the tested anchor lattice, while the closure pass isolates a one-sided smeared-dominant stable region and leaves no stable braid phase.
figures.usage_note: Prefer a two-panel editorial recomposition using existing outputs only.

figures.caption_draft: Branch-wise ablation summary for the clustered braid/smear split. Phase-corridor flattening gives the clean discriminative collapse, whereas geometry-overlap weakening and 0<->1 operator cross-coupling suppression act only as secondary supports.
figures.usage_note: Use the comparison matrix as primary and the braid-survival panel as secondary support.

figures.paper_role: final positive and negative claim ledger summary
figures.caption_draft: Frozen Phase III claim architecture. The positive core is limited to clustered-sector reduction, one-sided smeared closure, and phase-corridor-primary mechanism control, while family-wide braid claims and braid-phase closure remain excluded.
figures.usage_note: Prefer the positive and negative tables as a paired figure; use the mechanism-and-phase closure panel as the compact manuscript summary graphic.

editorial_constraints.scope_warning: Do not use figure captions that universalize clustered braid texture into a family-wide mechanism.
